results.title: Melt-Banana @ Instants Chavirés
results.overview: Melt-Banana are set to play two consecutive nights at Les Instants Chavirés on June 7 & 8, 2026 ! Not to be missed ! Melt-Banana (Tokyo) consists of Yasuko Onuki ("Yako") and guitarist Ichiro Agata ("Agata"). Agata's guitar sound is often described as a battery of turntables, synthesizers, and samples hidden between the six strings of the guitar, creating this "Hypertension Sound." Melt-Banana concerts leave a lasting impression : grindcore riffs loaded with unexpected effects, piercing screams, and intense pressure. Is Melt-Banana a noise band ? Or a punk band ? Techno Pop ? Giving Melt-Banana a brand identity is impossible. The group is synonymous with innovation and progress. Their sound stands apart and is often considered a precursor for many rock, noise, and experimental bands.

results.title: Red Planet 3009

We follow her searches, her discoveries, and ‘reflections’ as strange glitches, at times, show phantom structures. Severe damage after a sandstorm causes her to reboot, revealing that she has been active for centuries. The structures are ruins of a failed colonisation project. Accepting her role as a recorder, Serena activates her ‘black box’ beacon, an archive of the entire human genome, sending a final blueprint for humanity into deep space before powering down.

Photographs, archives, and fiction, hybridized with rotoscoping and digital reconstructions using photogrammetry, help Lily, the industrial software, increasingly approach human perception.

results.title: Igor Zidić: Are We Alive?

However, the journey he decides to take whilst writing said book will not only be a journey through time and space, but also a personal one, as Zidić reconnects with his own childhood self and realizes why he fell in love with writing once again.

results.title: Under a Million Stars
results.overview: Experts and homeless neighbors alike bust myths about Seattle’s homelessness crisis in a deeply reported, humanistic dispatch from the front lines. Homelessness was declared a civil state of emergency in Seattle in 2015. Since then, the number of people sleeping outside has actually risen by more than 67 percent. Chezik Tsunoda's documentary Under a Million Stars, which received the Satterberg Foundation's Courageous Documentary Filmmaking Grant, is a deeply reported yet humanistic dispatch from the front lines of this crisis. Armed with facts and empathy, the film busts pernicious and all-too-common myths about homelessness.

results.overview: 52 Pick Up is a comedy thriller about two young men, who form their relationship inside an apartment building, searching behind every door to uncover the truth. The first young man is a quirky P.I., whose job is synonymous with his life. After his terrible disappointment from a failed case, he persistently searches for the reason for his failure. Once he finds it, he decides to follow him to the apartment building of the other young man, Mitsos. Mitsos is an idle car mechanic, working in his father's business. His carefree life is turned upside down when a daily puzzle outside his own door seems to be trying to shake him up strongly. Through an allegory, audio documents, a pineapple juice, a near encounter with radishes, a key and a harlequin, Mitsos is called upon to keep his cool and untangle the tangle - always for his own good.

results.title: Go Fish 2
results.overview: Years after a game that was never meant to end, the cards are dealt once more.  What begins as a casual night of UNO among strangers is quietly interrupted by a man who has seen the game before… and survived it. As familiar patterns begin to resurface, the players find themselves slipping into roles that aren’t theirs—voices, memories, and unfinished conflicts bleeding through each turn.  Possession becomes participation. Guilt becomes gameplay.  And the past refuses to stay buried.  As the lines between player and pawn blur, one truth emerges:  Some games don’t end.  They wait.

results.overview: On the night before graduation at Portage Northern High School, four friends Carmen, Vig, Ace, and Aanya walk through the quiet halls of their school one last time. When they stop outside their classroom, they decide to sit together and talk about their memories from the past four years, remembering the moments that made their high school life special. As they reflect, scenes from the lives of other seniors appear in montages, showing the many experiences shared by the Class of 2026. When they finally leave the classroom, Vig pauses and sees a group of younger students running in, ready to begin their own journey. In that moment, he realizes that while their time at the school is ending, a new chapter is just beginning for others.

Far surpassing the traditional boundaries of union work, this extraordinary organization fosters solidarity, champions justice, and envisions a more equitable future for the working class.

results.title: DEAD DOGS DON'T BARK

results.title: 823rd Kilometer
results.overview: The elderly writer Rishat, who has achieved recognition, feels an imminent death and increasingly begins to remember his roots and goes to his small homeland and dedicates a new novel to her. The hero's son, Amir, a young successful Kazan film director, does not understand the strict and wayward father, who, in his opinion, "lives in the past", but wants to get at least some approval from him.

results.title: Colors of White Rock
results.overview: Ever since its shift toward liberalism after the Cold War, Mongolia has opened up its mines in the Gobi desert for exploitation — resulting in an onslaught of truck convoys transporting Mongolian coal to China. Within this arduous, predominantly-male line of work, Maikhuu — a former taxi driver and hairdresser — stands alone as one of the only female truck drivers working the mining boom. A single mother, Maikhuu resorts to looking after her young children’s financial future and well-being by working extensive seasons along the unforgiving Gobi desert, all while her sister cares for her young children back home. As an outspoken and resilient driver, Maikhuu contends not just with severe droughts impacting the desert landscape, but also deadly accidents among the truck drivers as well as incessant competition from her dominating male peers. Throughout it all, Maikhuu exerts a tenacity in her arduous work and outlook on life.

results.title: Aquella Primavera del 65
results.overview: Documentary about the women who participated in the revolution of April 1965 in Dominican Republic.

results.title: City Mark: A Rochester Icon
results.overview: A documentary film exploring the origin, evolution, and lasting impact of one of Rochester, NY's most recognizable symbols. From its creation in the 1970s to its place in today’s culture, the City Mark’s simple design became a powerful expression of identity, pride, and belonging. Through archival footage, local voices, and new interviews, the documentary traces the mark’s journey — from a government design initiative to a symbol embraced by the community. It’s a design story, but more importantly, it’s a story about people.

results.title: ARROGATION (Original Short Film)
results.overview: ’ARROGATION’ is a haunting, mind-breaking window into the life of a man working his hardest for a woman to get back into a society that controls and shunned her away for knowing the truth…

results.title: Eins Zwei Drei ‒ The Triplets Trio

results.title: Queen's Land
results.overview: Queen’s Land tells the oft forgotten history of drag, repression and resistance in Queensland’s queer scene during the Joh Bjelke-Petersen dictatorship. With glitter and glamour Destiny Rogers describes the ups and downs of life under Australia’s most conservative police state through the 60s to 80s. Petrified jelly, bribery, and gay bikies. Despite living in “pig city” a community was able to persist. To complement, Sel Dowd provides a history of the activist struggles of the era which inform our ongoing struggles today.

results.title: Distant Polyethylene Star
results.overview: Francis is struggling to grieve for his recently deceased cousin Marshall when a mysterious plastic container appears at his doorstep.

results.title: My home / House of Light
results.overview: The Kazuo Shinohara–designed "House on a Curved Road" was the home of my father, Shirouyasu Suzuki. After my father and mother passed away and the rooms were cleared, only light moves within my memories. By capturing this wavering light, the work is an attempt to grasp how the foundation of private expression exists through changes in the external world.

results.title: New Beginning

results.title: Spirit of The Valley
results.overview: “Spirit of the Valley” is a medium-length film composed entirely of fragments from an anonymous Super8 collection shot in the '80s. No new footage has been added.  The protagonists of this bio-photochemical impromptu jam session are the faces and places captured by the filmmaker’s lens during those years: scattered fragments that I selected and altered through various interventions on the emulsion, alongside the assistance of time.  Images shot almost half a century ago survive in an eroded state, resurfacing at times untouched in this ghost home movie, an elegy to 8mm film, an ode to Moviechrome, and a requiem for Kodachrome.

results.title: Fuggiamo lontano
results.overview: Developed with ten students from JFK in Monselice, drawn from their second and third year high school classes specializing in agricultural and computer science, Fuggiamo lontano (Let's Run Away) is a film based entirely on archive material that tells the story of a journey through four stories of teenagers who imagine going to the place of their dreams with a loved one by their side: their dog, their brother, their mother, and their friend. The voices of Sara, Matteo, Serena, and Alessandro intertwine with seemingly distant images, triggering a space-time collision in which feelings explode out of time.

results.title: How big, how blue, how beautiful
results.original_title: How big, how blue, how beautiful
results.overview: For me, places are people. Venice, tormented, fascinating, mysterious, is Giulio, his blue eyes, his disappearance, his ghost. There isn't a day that goes by that I don't think of the time his body was beside mine as we explored the island. Venice reawakens my memories with him and gives him back to me. Knowing that he has changed shape, that he no longer wears a human body, drives me to search for him everywhere. Yet I continue to believe in his desperate freedom, in his choice. The emptiness within me after his departure is a space I fill with what I encounter. I know only one way to combat pain: seek wonder. I will continue to do so, now also for him, now also with him.

results.title: Lupae
results.overview: Through the intertwined voices of three sex workers from Colombia, Chile, and Italy, this poetic and performative documentary unites their stories into a single poetic body, dissolving the boundaries of culture, history, and identity to reveal how the experiences of sex work are profoundly tied to the broader history of the female condition itself.

results.title: Hands Off
results.overview: During what should have been a simple job, two members of a trio of looters have yet to return to the getaway vehicle. The third member, Reed, tired of waiting, exits his vehicle and walks into the abandoned building to find his accomplices. However, once inside the dilapidated building, Reed soon learns that this evacuation mission may be harder than he initially thought.

results.title: I'm Fine
results.overview: When her friend calls to check in, Tori is asked a simple question. We hear everything she would say: the anxiety, the feeling of being trapped inside her own mind and body, the exhaustion of it all. But when the moment comes, she says nothing. She never does.

results.title: In Conversation with Nout en Linde
results.original_title: In Gesprek met Nout en Linde
results.overview: A conversation about transitioning.

results.title: October-January
results.overview: To get out of a slump, I started filming my household chores, but this ended up as a short film about those months, focusing mainly on the people around me.

results.title: Eye Swap
results.overview: A young woman living in a  dark forest is dissatisfied with her life and buys a pair of eyes  from a mysterious witch, which allows her to escape her own reality. However, the magical  effect of the eyes doesn’t last long and the woman soon finds herself forced to make a  choice with lasting consequences.

results.title: SLO Heist
results.overview: Special agent Vincent Kane is pulled back into action when a mysterious caller recruits him to carry out a heist at his very own high school.

results.overview: Animated short film

results.title: Sound Of Symbols
results.overview: Using a 16mm optical audio reader and an oscilloscope, this experimental short translates the geometry of everyday symbols into their characteristic sounds, creating visual music from the shapes that surround us.

results.overview: A satire film from Lawson Saby

results.title: Rescued
results.overview: A short film of the risky rescue mission at Ridglan Farms and the brave activists who carried it out, shot in real time by the documentary filmmaker Leighton Woodhouse.

results.title: Virescent Vengeance
results.overview: 2 partners invite a target to a fake meeting to assassinate the target, but the plan gets turned on its head when the target fights back

results.title: I Listen for the Sound of Ashes
results.original_title: I Listen for the Sound of Ashes
results.overview: I Listen for the Sound of Ashes unfolds through recorded phone conversations between the filmmaker and his parents during a period of political unrest in Nepal. As they speak about fear, memory, and everyday life, the screen holds quiet American landscapes: mountains, clouds, empty fields — places that feel still while everything back home feels unsettled. The film is about distance, but more than that, it’s about relationships: between a son and his parents, between diaspora and homeland, between safety and responsibility. It asks what happens to identity when you live far away from the place that shaped you, and how connection survives across that distance.

results.title: Girl From Nepal
results.overview: A girl from Nepal, Hema, comes to Kyrgyzstan and finds Kairat to give him a letter from her mother Uma. From the letter we learn that they met 40 years ago when Kairat mistook Uma for the Indian actress Hema Malini.

results.title: Unreachables
results.overview: Bishal decides to meet his friend, Mausam, one last time, before leaving Nepal. While sharing a drink, they talk about their life and family. They reminisce about their past and ponder over their uncertain futures. Both are not ready for new beginnings. As Bishal prepares to leave, Mausam stays with remnants of his past.

results.title: I Have Become a Rhododendron
results.overview: Maiya Devi Pandey, a 70-year-old Nepali woman and her grandson, bond over and discover each other through football.

results.title: Something Strange in the Dark
results.overview: A kid with insomnia starts to see a ghost at night.

results.title: Bonjour Nai Nai
results.overview: Leo lives in France. At his grandmother's Chinese funeral, he learns more about the culture of his ancestors.

results.title: Ultimatum
results.overview: Saran and Soleil are competing for a spot at the ballet school. But there's only room for one of them.

results.title: Nine Counties
results.overview: The Winter Solstice as seen through the lens of a Super 8 road movie; a vignette painted through impressions of the land, the sights, the sounds and the local radio stations.
